questionText: A snapshot $1\frac{7}{8}'' \times 1\frac{1}{2}''$ is to be enlarged so that the longer dimension is 4". What will be the dimension of the shorter side?

steps.explanation: Original dimensions: longer = $1\frac{7}{8}'' = \frac{15}{8}''$, shorter = $1\frac{1}{2}'' = \frac{3}{2}''$.
Apply scale factor to shorter side.
Using the chain-rule proportion: more length, more breadth (Direct).

solution.shortcut: Scale ratio = new longer / old longer; apply same ratio to shorter side.
solution.commonMistake: Confusing which is the longer vs shorter dimension.

questionText: A canteen requires 651 bananas for a week. Totally, how many bananas will it require for the months of April, May and June?

steps.explanation: April has 30 days, May has 31 days, June has 30 days. Total = 91 days.
Total bananas = 651 per week × 13 weeks.

solution.commonMistake: Forgetting that April and June have 30 days each.

questionText: If $\frac{4}{9}$ of a bucket is filled in 1 minute, the rest of it will be filled in

steps.explanation: Remaining part = $1 - \frac{4}{9} = \frac{5}{9}$.
More volume to be filled, more time (Direct Proportion). $\frac{4}{9}$ takes 1 min, so $\frac{5}{9}$ takes x min.

solution.shortcut: Remaining fraction / filled fraction = remaining time.
solution.commonMistake: Using the full bucket instead of the remaining part.

questionText: On a certain map of India the actual distance of 1450 km between two cities Delhi and Kolkata is shown as 5 cm. What scale is used to draw the map?

steps.explanation: Convert actual distance to cm: 1450 km = 1450 × 1000 × 100 cm = 145,000,000 cm.
Scale = map distance : actual distance = 5 : 145,000,000.

solution.shortcut: Convert km to cm (multiply by 10⁵), then divide by map cm.
solution.commonMistake: Not converting km to cm before computing scale.

questionText: A flagstaff 17.5 m high casts a shadow of length 40.25 m. The height of the building, which casts a shadow of length 28.75 m under similar conditions will be

steps.explanation: Less lengthy shadow → less height (Direct Proportion).

solution.shortcut: Height ∝ Shadow length under same conditions.
solution.commonMistake: Setting up inverse proportion instead of direct.

questionText: A TV tower 36 metres high casts a shadow of 24 metres at a particular time of a day. What is the height of a minar with a three metre high flagstaff atop it, if both of these together cast a shadow of 50 metres at the same time of the day?

steps.explanation: Let the height of the minar with flagstaff be x metres. More shadow → more height (Direct Proportion).
Height of minar alone = 75 − 3 = 72 m.

solution.shortcut: Find total height using proportion, then subtract flagstaff height.
solution.commonMistake: Forgetting to subtract the 3 m flagstaff height.

questionText: A man completes $\frac{5}{8}$ of a job in 10 days. At this rate, how many more days will it take him to finish the job?

steps.explanation: Remaining work = $1 - \frac{5}{8} = \frac{3}{8}$.
Rate = $\frac{5/8}{10} = \frac{1}{16}$ per day. Time for $\frac{3}{8}$: $\frac{3/8}{1/16} = 6$ days.

solution.shortcut: Remaining fraction / done fraction × days taken.
solution.commonMistake: Finding total days instead of additional days.

questionText: 56 men can complete a piece of work in 24 days. In how many days can 42 men complete the same piece of work?

steps.explanation: Less men → more days (Indirect Proportion).

solution.shortcut: Men × Days = constant; $42x = 56 \times 24$.
solution.commonMistake: Using direct proportion instead of indirect.

questionText: 30 men can do a piece of work in 16 days. How many men would be required to do the same work in 20 days?

steps.explanation: More days → less men (Indirect Proportion). $20 : 16 :: 30 : x$.

solution.commonMistake: Applying direct proportion (more days → more men) incorrectly.

questionText: A group of workers promise to complete a piece of work in 10 days, but five of them do not report for work. If it took the remaining workers 12 days to complete the work, then the number of workers originally hired was

steps.explanation: Let original workers = x. After 5 leave, (x−5) workers take 12 days. Less workers → more days (Indirect Proportion).

solution.shortcut: $(x-5) \times 12 = x \times 10$.
solution.commonMistake: Setting up $(x-5):x :: 12:10$ (swapping proportion sides).

questionText: A wheel that has 6 cogs is meshed with a larger wheel of 14 cogs. When the smaller wheel has made 21 revolutions, then the number of revolutions made by the larger wheel is

steps.explanation: More cogs → fewer revolutions (Indirect Proportion). $14 : 6 :: 21 : x$.

solution.shortcut: Cogs × Revolutions = constant for meshed wheels.
solution.commonMistake: Using direct proportion (more cogs → more revolutions).

questionText: In a camp, there is a meal for 120 men or 200 children. If 150 children have taken the meal, how many men will be catered to with the remaining meal?

steps.explanation: 200 children ≡ 120 men. Remaining children after 150 eat = 200 − 150 = 50 children worth of food.

solution.shortcut: Remaining fraction of total food × men capacity.
solution.commonMistake: Not computing remaining food correctly.

questionText: The cost of 16 packets of salt, each weighing 900 grams is ₹28. What will be the cost of 27 packets, if each packet weighs 1 kg?

steps.explanation: More packets → more cost (Direct); more weight → more cost (Direct).

solution.shortcut: Two-variable chain rule: cost scales with both packets and weight.
solution.commonMistake: Forgetting to account for the weight difference (900 g vs 1000 g).

questionText: 4 mat-weavers can weave 4 mats in 4 days. At the same rate, how many mats would be woven by 8 mat-weavers in 8 days?

steps.explanation: More weavers → more mats (Direct); more days → more mats (Direct).

solution.shortcut: Mats ∝ Weavers × Days. Doubling both doubles in each dimension: $4 \times 2 \times 2 = 16$.
solution.commonMistake: Thinking answer is 8 by doubling only once.

questionText: If 7 maids with 7 mops cleaned 7 floors in 7 hours, how long would it take 3 maids to mop 3 floors with 3 mops?

steps.explanation: 1 maid + 1 mop = 1 unit. 7 units clean 7 floors in 7 hours. Less units → more time (Indirect); less floors → less time (Direct).

solution.shortcut: The ratio of maids/floors cancels; time stays 7 hours.
solution.commonMistake: Only applying one proportion and ignoring the other.

modelName: Chain Rule — Indirect and Direct Proportions

formulas.title: Chain Rule
formulas.expression: W_1 \cdot F_1 \cdot T_1 = W_2 \cdot F_2 \cdot T_2 \text{ (adjusted for type)}

questionText: Four gardeners with four grass mowers mow 400 sq. m of ground in 4 hours. How long would it take for eight gardeners with eight grass mowers to mow 800 sq. m of ground?

steps.explanation: 1 gardener + 1 mower = 1 unit. 4 units → 400 sq.m in 4 hrs. More units → less time (Indirect); more area → more time (Direct).

solution.shortcut: Doubling gardeners/mowers halves time; doubling area doubles time — effects cancel.
solution.commonMistake: Adding both effects (doubling) to get 8 hours.

modelName: Chain Rule — Indirect and Direct Proportions

formulas.title: Chain Rule
formulas.expression: G \cdot A \cdot x = G_0 \cdot A_0 \cdot T_0 \text{ (adjusted)}

questionText: Running at the same constant rate, 6 identical machines can produce a total of 180 bottles per hour. How many bottles could 15 such machines produce in 30 minutes?

steps.explanation: More machines → more bottles (Direct); less time → less bottles (Direct).

solution.shortcut: 180 bottles/hr = 3 per min per 6 machines = 0.5/machine/min; 15 × 30 × 0.5 = 225.
solution.commonMistake: Forgetting to convert 30 min vs 60 min (1 hour) for rate.

questionText: If 6 persons working 8 hours a day earn ₹8400 per week, then 9 persons working 6 hours a day will earn per week

steps.explanation: More persons → more earning (Direct); less hours → less earning (Direct).

solution.shortcut: $x = 8400 \times \frac{9}{6} \times \frac{6}{8} = 8400 \times \frac{9}{8} = 9450$.
solution.commonMistake: Not accounting for both persons and hours in the proportion.

modelName: Chain Rule — Earnings with Persons and Hours

questionText: If 5 workers can collect 60 kg wheat in 3 days, how many kilograms of wheat will 8 workers collect in 5 days?

steps.explanation: More workers → more wheat (Direct); more days → more wheat (Direct).

solution.shortcut: $\frac{W_2}{W_1} = \frac{8 \times 5}{5 \times 3}$; $W_2 = 60 \times \frac{8}{3} = 160$.
solution.commonMistake: Forgetting to scale by both workers and days.

questionText: 50 people consume 350 kg of rice in 30 days. In how many days will 35 people consume 50 kg of rice?

steps.explanation: Less people → more days (Indirect); less quantity → fewer days (Direct).

solution.shortcut: Apply chain rule: Indirect for people, Direct for quantity.
solution.commonMistake: Treating both factors as direct proportions.

modelName: Chain Rule — Indirect and Direct Proportions

formulas.title: Chain Rule
formulas.expression: P_1 Q_1 D_1 = P_2 Q_2 D_2 \text{ (adjusted for types)}

questionText: In a dairy farm, 40 cows eat 40 bags of husk in 40 days. In how many days one cow will eat one bag of husk?

steps.explanation: Less cows → more days (Indirect); less husk → less days (Direct).

solution.shortcut: Each cow eats 1 bag in 40 days — the '40s' cancel except for days.
solution.commonMistake: Thinking all three 40s multiply to give a large number.

modelName: Chain Rule — Indirect and Direct Proportions

formulas.title: Chain Rule
formulas.expression: C_1 D_1 B_1 = C_2 D_2 B_2 \text{ (adjusted)}

questionText: Working 8 hours a day, 12 men can do a work in 30 days. Working 4 hours a day, 18 men can do the work in

steps.explanation: Total work = $12 \times 8 \times 30 = 2880$ man-hours.
Days for 18 men at 4 hrs/day: $D = \frac{2880}{18 \times 4} = \frac{2880}{72} = 40$.

solution.shortcut: More men cuts time but fewer hours per day increases it: $30 \times \frac{12}{18} \times \frac{8}{4} = 40$.
solution.commonMistake: Not accounting for hours per day in addition to number of men.

questionText: 12 men working 8 hours per day complete a piece of work in 10 days. To complete the same work in 8 days, working 15 hours a day, the number of men required, is:

steps.explanation: Less days → more men (Indirect); more hrs/day → less men (Indirect).

solution.shortcut: $x = 12 \times \frac{10}{8} \times \frac{8}{15} = 8$.
solution.commonMistake: Treating hours as direct proportion instead of indirect.

questionText: 5 persons can prepare an admission list in 8 days working 7 hours a day. If 2 persons join them so as to complete the work in 4 days, they need to work per day for

steps.explanation: Total person-hours = $5 \times 8 \times 7 = 280$.
New group: 5+2=7 persons, 4 days, x hours/day. $7 \times 4 \times x = 280$.

solution.shortcut: $x = \frac{5 \times 8 \times 7}{7 \times 4} = 10$.
solution.commonMistake: Forgetting to add 2 to get 7 total persons.

questionText: 3 pumps, working 8 hours a day, can empty a tank in 2 days. How many hours a day must 4 pumps work to empty the tank in 1 day?

steps.explanation: More pumps → less hrs/day (Indirect); less days → more hrs/day (Indirect).

solution.shortcut: $x = \frac{3 \times 2 \times 8}{4 \times 1} = 12$.
solution.commonMistake: Treating pumps or days as direct proportion.

questionText: If 8 men can reap 80 hectares in 24 days, then how many hectares can 36 men reap in 30 days?

steps.explanation: More men → more hectares (Direct); more days → more hectares (Direct).

solution.shortcut: $x = 80 \times \frac{36}{8} \times \frac{30}{24} = 80 \times 4.5 \times 1.25 = 450$.
solution.commonMistake: Forgetting to scale by both men and days.

questionText: A certain number of persons can dig a trench 100 m long, 50 m broad and 10 m deep in 10 days. The same number of persons can dig another trench 20 m broad and 15 m deep in 30 days. The length of the second trench is

steps.explanation: Volume of first trench = $100 \times 50 \times 10 = 50000$ cu.m done in 10 days.
Less breadth → more length (Indirect); more depth → less length (Indirect); more days → more length (Direct).

solution.shortcut: $x = 100 \times \frac{50}{20} \times \frac{10}{15} \times \frac{30}{10} = 100 \times 2.5 \times \frac{2}{3} \times 3 = 500$.
solution.commonMistake: Not applying indirect proportion for breadth and depth.

questionText: If 5 men or 7 women can earn ₹5250 per day, how much would 7 men and 13 women earn per day?

steps.explanation: 5 men ≡ 7 women ⇒ 1 man ≡ $\frac{7}{5}$ women. 7 men ≡ $\frac{49}{5}$ women.
7 women earn ₹5250. So $\frac{114}{5}$ women earn $\frac{5250}{7} \times \frac{114}{5} = 750 \times \frac{114}{5} = 150 \times 114 = 17100$.

solution.shortcut: Convert men to women-equivalent, then use direct proportion with earnings.
solution.commonMistake: Not converting men to equivalent women before applying proportion.

questionText: 3 men or 6 women can do a piece of work in 20 days. In how many days will 12 men and 8 women do the same work?

steps.explanation: 3 men ≡ 6 women ⇒ 1 man ≡ 2 women. 12 men ≡ 24 women.
6 women take 20 days; 32 women take x days. More women → less days (Indirect).

solution.shortcut: Convert all to one unit (women), then use inverse proportion.
solution.commonMistake: Not converting men to women-equivalent first.

modelName: Chain Rule — Equivalence and Indirect Proportion

questionText: If 5 men or 9 women can do a piece of work in 19 days, then in how many days will 3 men and 6 women do the same work?

steps.explanation: 5 men ≡ 9 women ⇒ 3 men ≡ $\frac{9 \times 3}{5} = \frac{27}{5}$ women.
$\frac{57}{5} : 9 :: 19 : x \Rightarrow x = \frac{9 \times 19 \times 5}{57} = \frac{855}{57} = 15$.

solution.shortcut: Convert men to women-equivalent, then apply inverse proportion.
solution.commonMistake: Not reducing to a common unit before applying proportion.

modelName: Chain Rule — Equivalence and Indirect Proportion

questionText: 49 pumps can empty a reservoir in $6\frac{1}{2}$ days, working 8 hours a day. If 196 pumps are used for 5 hours each day, then the same work will be completed in

steps.explanation: More pumps → less days (Indirect); less hrs/day → more days (Indirect).

solution.shortcut: $x = \frac{49}{196} \times \frac{8}{5} \times \frac{13}{2} = \frac{1}{4} \times \frac{8}{5} \times \frac{13}{2} = \frac{13}{5} = 2.6$.
solution.commonMistake: Not converting $6\frac{1}{2}$ to $\frac{13}{2}$ before computing.

questionText: 30 labourers, working 7 hours a day can finish a piece of work in 18 days. If the labourers work 6 hours a day, then the number of labourers to finish the same piece of work in 30 days, will be:

steps.explanation: Less hrs/day → more labourers (Indirect); more days → less labourers (Indirect).

solution.shortcut: $x = 30 \times \frac{7}{6} \times \frac{18}{30} = 30 \times \frac{7}{6} \times \frac{3}{5} = 21$.
solution.commonMistake: Not correctly identifying which variables are direct vs indirect.

questionText: If 18 pumps can raise 2170 tonnes of water in 10 days, working 7 hours a day; in how many days will 16 pumps raise 1736 tonnes of water, working 9 hours a day?

steps.explanation: Less pumps → more days (Indirect); less weight → less days (Direct); more hrs/day → less days (Indirect).

solution.shortcut: Apply three-factor chain rule: pumps (indirect), weight (direct), hours (indirect).
solution.commonMistake: Confusing direct and indirect proportion for pumps vs. weight.

formulas.title: Chain Rule
formulas.expression: P_1 W_1 H_1 D_1 = P_2 W_2 H_2 D_2 \text{ (adjusted)}

questionText: If 80 lamps can be lighted, 5 hours per day for 10 days for ₹21.25, then the number of lamps, which can be lighted 4 hours daily for 30 days, for ₹76.50, is

steps.explanation: More cost → more lamps (Direct); less hours → less lamps (Direct); more days → more lamps (Direct).
Setting up correctly: lamps ∝ cost, lamps ∝ hours, lamps ∝ days (all direct). $\frac{x}{80} = \frac{76.50}{21.25} \times \frac{4}{5} \times \frac{30}{10}$.

solution.shortcut: $x = 80 \times \frac{76.5}{21.25} \times \frac{4}{5} \times \frac{30}{10} = 80 \times \frac{306}{85} \times \frac{4}{5} \times 3 = 120$.
solution.commonMistake: Treating cost as inverse of lamps.

questionText: If 12 carpenters, working 6 hours a day, can make 460 chairs in 24 days, how many chairs will 18 carpenters make in 36 days, each working 8 hours a day?

steps.explanation: More carpenters, hours/day, days → more chairs (all Direct).

solution.shortcut: $x = 460 \times \frac{18}{12} \times \frac{8}{6} \times \frac{36}{24} = 460 \times 1.5 \times \frac{4}{3} \times 1.5 = 1380$.
solution.commonMistake: Missing one of the three proportional factors.

questionText: If 5 spiders can catch five flies in five minutes, how many flies can hundred spiders catch in 100 minutes?

steps.explanation: More spiders → more flies (Direct); more time → more flies (Direct).

solution.shortcut: $x = 5 \times \frac{100}{5} \times \frac{100}{5} = 5 \times 20 \times 20 = 2000$.
solution.commonMistake: Answering 100 by only scaling spiders and not time.

questionText: 2 persons working 2 hours a day assemble 2 machines in 2 days. The number of machines assembled by 6 persons working 6 hours a day in 6 days is

steps.explanation: All three factors (persons, hours, days) are direct proportions for machines output.

solution.shortcut: $x = 2 \times \left(\frac{6}{2}\right)^3 = 2 \times 27 = 54$.
solution.commonMistake: Only tripling once (getting 6) instead of three-fold scaling.

questionText: The work done by a man is double the work done by a woman in the same time. If 10 men can do a piece of work in 8 days, then in how many days that work can be done by 3 men and 4 women?

steps.explanation: 1 man = 2 women. So 2 women ≡ 1 man ⇒ 4 women ≡ 2 men.
Less men → more days (Indirect). $5 : 10 :: 8 : x$.

solution.shortcut: Convert to men-equivalent, then use $M_1 D_1 = M_2 D_2$.
solution.commonMistake: Not converting women to men before applying inverse proportion.

modelName: Chain Rule — Equivalence and Indirect Proportion

questionText: A wall of 100 metres can be built by 7 men or 10 women in 10 days. How many days will 14 men and 20 women take to build a wall of 600 metres?

steps.explanation: 7 men ≡ 10 women ⇒ 14 men ≡ 20 women. So (14 men + 20 women) ≡ 40 women.
More length → more days (Direct); more women → less days (Indirect). $100 \times 40 \times x = 600 \times 10 \times 10$.

solution.shortcut: Convert to women-equivalent, apply chain rule with length (direct) and workers (indirect).
solution.commonMistake: Not converting 14 men to women-equivalent before applying proportion.

modelName: Chain Rule — Equivalence, Direct and Indirect

questionText: If 10 men or 20 boys can make 260 mats in 20 days, then how many mats will be made by 8 men and 4 boys in 20 days?

steps.explanation: 10 men ≡ 20 boys ⇒ 1 man ≡ 2 boys ⇒ 8 men ≡ 16 boys.
20 boys in 20 days make 260 mats (same as original 20 boys). Answer = 260.

solution.shortcut: 8 men + 4 boys = 20 boys equivalent = same as original group ⇒ same output.
solution.commonMistake: Not recognizing the equivalence leads to same capacity.

questionText: If 600 men dig a 5.5 m wide, 4 m deep and 405 m long canal in half an hour, then how long a canal will 2500 men, working for 6 hours, dig if it is 10 m wide and 8 m deep?

steps.explanation: More men → more length (Direct); more width → less length (Indirect); more depth → less length (Indirect); more time → more length (Direct).

solution.shortcut: Apply four-factor chain rule carefully identifying direct vs indirect for each dimension.
solution.commonMistake: Forgetting to convert half-hour = 0.5 hours or mixing up direct/indirect for dimensions.

questionText: 64 persons can dig a trench 50 m long, 2 m wide and 2 m deep in 5 days, working 12 hours daily. In how many days, working 8 hours daily, will 80 persons dig another trench 75 m long, 4 m wide and 3 m deep?

steps.explanation: Persons (Indirect), Length/Width/Depth (Direct), Working hrs (Indirect) for days.

solution.shortcut: $x = 5 \times \frac{64}{80} \times \frac{75}{50} \times \frac{4}{2} \times \frac{3}{2} \times \frac{12}{8} = 5 \times 0.8 \times 1.5 \times 2 \times 1.5 \times 1.5 = 27$.
solution.commonMistake: Confusing direct and indirect proportion for persons and working hours.

questionText: 21 binders can bind 1400 books in 15 days. How many binders will be required to bind 800 books in 20 days?

steps.explanation: Less books → less binders (Direct); more days → less binders (Indirect).

solution.shortcut: $x = 21 \times \frac{800}{1400} \times \frac{15}{20} = 21 \times \frac{4}{7} \times \frac{3}{4} = 9$.
solution.commonMistake: Treating days as direct (more days → more binders).

modelName: Chain Rule — Direct and Indirect Proportions

formulas.title: Chain Rule
formulas.expression: B_1 D_1 b_1 = B_2 D_2 b_2 \text{ (adjusted)}

questionText: A certain number of artisans can complete a shoe fabrication consignment in 16 days. 8 additional artisans had to be deployed for the same consignment and together they completed it in 4 days less than the earlier estimate. The number of artisans initially employed was

steps.explanation: Let original artisans = x. They complete in 16 days; (x+8) complete in 12 days. More artisans → less days (Indirect).

solution.shortcut: $(x+8) \times 12 = x \times 16$.
solution.commonMistake: Using direct proportion instead of indirect.

questionText: If 9 examiners can examine a certain number of answer books in 12 days, working 5 hours a day; for how many hours a day would 4 examiners have to work in order to examine twice the number of answer books in 30 days?

steps.explanation: Less examiners → more hrs/day (Indirect); more days → less hrs/day (Indirect); more answer books → more hrs/day (Direct).

solution.shortcut: $x = 5 \times \frac{9}{4} \times \frac{12}{30} \times \frac{2}{1} = 5 \times \frac{9}{4} \times \frac{2}{5} \times 2 = 9$.
solution.commonMistake: Not correctly identifying direct vs indirect for each factor.

formulas.title: Chain Rule
formulas.expression: E_1 D_1 B_1 H_1 = E_2 D_2 B_2 H_2 \text{ (adjusted)}
